name: Dolly Dawn
abstract: Dolly Dawn (born Theresa Anna Maria Stabile; February 3, 1916 – December 11, 2002) was an American big band singer. She was vocalist with George Hall's Hotel Taft Orchestra in the 1930s, and later had a solo career.

concerts.label: Afternoon at Carnegie Hall to Benefit Music for the Blind

about_quotes.speaker: Butch Miles
about_quotes.evidence: I mean I recorded with Basie, recorded with Mulligan, recorded with Alberta Hunter. Here’s a name for you, Dolly Dawn, there’s one, remember that one.
about_quotes.doc_title: Butch Miles interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 9, 2020 [interview 2 of 2]
